Notice Of Motion To Strike Defendant Pamela Manicks Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Aug-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEXANDRIA STATHOPOULOS OF ORRICK, HERRINGTON & STUCLIFFE, TEL. NO. 1-415-773-5635 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS PG&E AND LINA BINNS;
ATTY. ANDREW M. MASSARA OF LONG & LEVIT, TEL. NO. 1-415-397-2222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PAMELA MANICK.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JULIE FAUSTINO'S Motion To Strike Defendant Pamela Manicks Memorandum Of Costs is DENIED. The evidence does not establish an actual affirmative agreement between the parties that Defendant would not seek costs in return for Plaintiff's voluntary dismissal of the action. Costs of $4,139 awarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb

Notice Of Motion To Tax Defendant Pges Memorandum Of Costs
Off Calendar Aug-25-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. ALEXANDRIA STATHOPOULOS OF ORRICK, HERRINGTON & STUCLIFFE, TEL. NO. 1-415-773-5635 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS PG&E AND LINA BINNS;
ATTY. ANDREW M. MASSARA OF LONG & LEVIT, TEL. NO. 1-415-397-2222 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT PAMELA MANICK.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF JULIE FAUSTINO'S Motion To Tax Defendant PG&E's Memorandum Of Costs is DENIED. Deposition subpoena fees for healthcare providers are recoverable costs. (See Naser v. Lakeridge Athletic Club (2014) 227 Cal.App.4th 571, 576-78.) Plaintiff does not provide any support for her assertion that PG&Es deposition costs are outrageous and unreasonable. Plaintiff fails to carry her burden to show these costs are unreasonable. (See Nelson v. Anderson (1999) 72 Cal.App.4th 111, 131.) Costs of $7,176.85 awarded. Order signed in open court. Judge: Suzanne Bolanos; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/sb

Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Judgment By Defendants Linda Binns, Pacific Gas And Electric Company (Part 2 Of 2)
Off Calendar May-26-2015 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Judgment By Defendants Linda Binns, Pacific Gas And Electric Company (Part 2 Of 2)

Duplicate entry for purposes of tentative ruling only. (D302)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar May-26-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tara J. Macomber, appearing via CourtCall, of LAW OFFICES OF TARA J. MACOMBER, Tel. 510.388.4949, for plaintiffs Julie Faustino;
Douglas Milton, of LONG LEVIT LLP, Tel. 415.397.2222, for defendant Pamela Manick;
Andrew R. Livingston, of ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP, Tel. 415.773.5588, for defendants PG & E and Binns;
Craig Wickersham, of ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP, Tel. 415.773.5588, for defendants PG & E and Binns.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT LINA BINNS, PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is granted. Order signed in open court. (See word minutes for complete ruling). Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT And Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication Of Pg&E And Lina Binns (Joinder)
Off Calendar May-26-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tara J. Macomber, appearing via CourtCall, of LAW OFFICES OF TARA J. MACOMBER, Tel. 510.388.4949, for plaintiffs Julie Faustino;
Douglas Milton, of LONG LEVIT LLP, Tel. 415.397.2222, for defendant Pamela Manick;
Andrew R. Livingston, of ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP, Tel. 415.773.5588, for defendants PG & E and Binns;
Craig Wickersham, of ORRICK, HERRINGTON & SUTCLIFFE LLP, Tel. 415.773.5588, for defendants PG & E and Binns.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Granted as to the harassment cause of action. The evidence establishes that Defendant's alleged conduct was not "severe" or "pervasive." (Undisputed Material Fact ("UMF") 11-12.) Further, there is no evidence presented to raise a triable issue of material fact as to whether there is a nexus between the alleged harassing conduct and Plaintiff's age. (See Crawford v. Medina General Hospital (1996) 96 F.3d 830, 836.) It is undisputed that Plaintiff told the Corporate Security investigator that Manick had not harassed her on the basis of age. (UMF 26.) Granted as to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. There is no evidence that any of Defendant's actions went beyond "mere insults, indignities, threats, annoyances, petty oppressions, or other trivialities." (Alcorn v. Anbro Eng., Inc. (1970) 2 Cal.3d 493, 499.) Denied as to the defamation per quod and the defamation per se causes of action. Defendant met her initial burden by introducing evidence showing her statement was one of opinion. (UMF 23-26.) Plaintiff raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Defendant knew that Plaintiff had not been fired at the time the statement was made. (Plaintiff's response to UMF 23.) The Court did not consider Defendant's argument about Plaintiff being unable to establish damages because it was raised for the first time on reply. Plaintiff's counsel shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. (See word minutes for complete ruling). Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
